pages.name: How the Maps Ship (and How to Dress Them)

text.content:
Ashen ships blank, grid-ready placeholder scenes — correct size, square 5-ft grid, mood-tinted background — but no map art. That keeps the module copyright-safe (no FromSoftware imagery) and lets you use whatever battlemaps you like. Every scene also carries a one-paragraph layout brief on its flags.ashen.brief, summarised on the next page.

Running it bare

You can play on the blank grid as-is: it's sized per encounter, the squares are 5 ft, and token vision/fog are on. Narrate the terrain from the brief and drop tokens. For a one-shot, this is totally fine.

Dropping in a battlemap (2 minutes)

  1. Find a map image (sources below). Note its pixel size.
  2. Open the scene → Configure (gear) → Background: set the image as the background source.
  3. On the Dimensions tab, set Width and Height to the image's pixels, then adjust Grid Size so one square lands on one map square (use the grid-alignment tool). Keep Grid Distance = 5 ft.
  4. Save. Re-place any tokens. Done.

Tip: if a map is already gridded at 70 px/square, set Grid Size to 70 rather than rescaling the image.

Default placeholder sizes (square 5-ft grid). Match your art to roughly these square counts for a clean fit.

SceneSquaresPixelsThe space
0 · Region Overviewgridless3200×2400Between-scene map; show the dragon in sight, orient backtracking.
1 · Cemetery / Gundyr30×243000×2400Grave slope funnels into a circular arena; pillars for cover on approach.
2 · Firelink Hub28×222800×2200Safe hub; Fire Keeper, Handmaid, Andre, Sword Master, central bonfire.
3 · High Wall / Courtyard36×283600×2800Entry hall → farm courtyard; hollows, archers on ramparts, hound pack.
4 · Dragon Bridge40×204000×2000Long exposed span; mark fire lanes, cover, and the dead zone under the perch.
5 · Outrider Arena26×262600×2600Tight enclosed duel; 1–2 pillars to break charge lanes, nowhere to turtle.
6 · Pus Wall & Shortcut30×223000×2200Rampart walk with ledges; corpse erupts; a kicked ladder opens the shortcut.
7 · Vordt Arena30×303000×3000Fog gate → long frozen hall; room to run the Frost Charge; victory bonfire beyond.

The full encounter detail (enemy placement, cover, the three Dragon solutions, the Teaching-Death beats) lives in the DM Codex journal and on each scene's brief flag.

A curated starting list for grim, ruined, snowy, castle-rampart maps and dark-fantasy tokens. Always check the individual asset's licence before using it — terms vary by creator, and some free tiers are personal-use or CC-BY-NC (fine for a private, non-monetised game; credit where asked).

Battlemaps

  • Dyson Logos (dysonlogos.blog) — huge free dungeon/keep library, commercial-friendly with credit. Great for ruined halls and ramparts.
  • 2-Minute Tabletop — free + paid maps and map-making assets; dark-fantasy and castle sets.
  • Forgotten Adventures — enormous free map & asset library (CC-BY-NC); excellent grim/ruined/snow tilesets and pre-made maps.
  • Cartography Assets (cartographyassets.com) — filter by free; many CC maps and brushes.
  • r/battlemaps & r/dndmaps — lots of free maps; read each poster's usage note.
  • Czepeku (free samples) & Tom Cartos — cinematic maps incl. snowy keeps and bridges (mostly paid; some free).

Tokens & portraits

  • Forgotten Adventures Tokens — thousands of free top-down tokens (undead, knights, beasts) fitting the hollow/knight roster.
  • Game-icons.net (CC-BY 3.0) — the same icon set Foundry uses; perfect for item cards, abilities, and minimalist tokens.
  • Foundry core art (the icons/ paths already used throughout this module) — ships with Foundry, safe to use.
  • Token Stamp 2 (rolladvantage) — free tool to frame any image into a round token.

Make your own (optional)

  • Dungeondraft (paid, one-time) — quick custom battlemaps; pairs well with Forgotten Adventures asset packs.
  • Inkarnate / Wonderdraft — region/overview maps for Scene 0.

None of these are FromSoftware assets. Use them to evoke Lothric's mood without shipping copyrighted art in the module.
